# histos

> Pack any web app — WASM, CSS, JS, HTML — into a single self-contained `index.html` file. Assets are brotli-compressed, base64-encoded, and inlined. The output requires no server, no CDN, and no internet connection to run.

## What it does

histos is a CLI tool that reads a YAML config file describing your web assets and produces a single portable `index.html`. WASM binaries are brotli-compressed and base64-encoded inside `<pre>` tags; the bundled runtime decoder unpacks them at load time using IndexedDB for caching. CSS files are concatenated and inlined. JavaScript and HTML fragments are inlined verbatim. Favicons are base64-encoded SVGs. The result is a fully offline, browser-runnable application in one file.

## Install

```bash
cargo install histos
```

Requires Rust + Cargo. To compile WASM from source, also install `wasm-pack`.

## CLI Usage

```bash
# Pack with default output (./index.html)
histos config.yaml

# Pack with explicit output path
histos config.yaml --output ./dist/index.html
histos config.yaml -o ./dist/index.html
```

## YAML Config Schema

The config file has a single top-level key `pack`. All sub-keys are optional unless noted.

```
pack:
  runtime:    RuntimeConfig       # controls which runtime assets are injected
  metadata:   MetadataConfig      # HTML <head> metadata
  favicon:    List[String]        # SVG favicon paths or URLs
  css:        List[String]        # CSS file paths or URLs (concatenated in order)
  html:       List[String]        # HTML fragment paths or URLs (inlined in order)
  scripts:    List[String]        # JS file paths or URLs (inlined in order)
  wasm:       Map[String → WasmModule]   # named WASM modules
```

### `pack.runtime`

Controls which default runtime components are injected into the HTML output.

| Key       | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `enabled` | bool | `true`  | Master switch. `false` disables all runtime injection and overrides other keys. |
| `icon`    | bool | `true`  | Inject the default SVG loading icon. |
| `core`    | bool | `true`  | Inject `core.js` — the main runtime loader that finds and executes the WASM module. |
| `decoder` | bool | `true`  | Inject the brotli WASM decoder (`wasm_decoder.js` + `wasm_decoder_bg.wasm`). Required when using `compression: "brotli"`. |

### `pack.metadata`

HTML `<head>` metadata. All fields are optional strings. Defaults: `title` = `"histos"`, rest = empty.

| Key           | Type   | Description |
|---------------|--------|-------------|
| `title`       | String | `<title>` and `<meta name="title">` |
| `author`      | String | `<meta name="author">` |
| `description` | String | `<meta name="description">` |
| `keywords`    | String | `<meta name="keywords">` |

### `pack.favicon`

List of local file paths or HTTP/HTTPS URLs to SVG files. Each is base64-encoded and injected as `<link rel="icon">`.

```yaml
favicon:
  - ./assets/icon.svg
  - https://example.com/icon.svg
```

### `pack.css`

List of local file paths or HTTP/HTTPS URLs to CSS files. All are fetched and concatenated into a single `<style>` block.

```yaml
css:
  - ./styles/main.css
  - https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/normalize/8.0.1/normalize.min.css
```

### `pack.html`

List of local file paths or HTTP/HTTPS URLs to HTML fragment files. Inlined verbatim into the document body.

```yaml
html:
  - ./fragments/canvas.html
```

### `pack.scripts`

List of local file paths or HTTP/HTTPS URLs to JavaScript files. Inlined verbatim inside `<script>` tags.

```yaml
scripts:
  - ./src/init.js
```

### `pack.wasm.<name>`

A map of named WASM modules. The map key (e.g. `module`, `app`) is only used internally for YAML grouping — it does not affect the output. The `id` field is what the runtime uses to locate the module.

| Key            | Type   | Default          | Description |
|----------------|--------|------------------|-------------|
| `path`         | String | —                | Path to the wasm-pack project directory (parent of `pkg/`). If set, `binary` and `glue` are auto-discovered from `<path>/pkg/`. Mutually exclusive with explicit `binary`/`glue`. |
| `binary`       | String | —                | Path or URL to the `.wasm` file. Used when `path` is not set. |
| `glue`         | String | —                | Path or URL to the JS glue file. Used when `path` is not set. |
| `id`           | String | `"bin-wasm-app"` | ID injected into the HTML. **Must be `"bin-wasm-app"`** when using the default runtime (`runtime.core = true`), because `core.js` looks for this ID to load the module. |
| `compile_wasm` | bool   | `false`          | If `true`, runs `wasm-pack build --target no-modules` before packing. Requires `wasm-pack` on PATH. |
| `compression`  | String | `"brotli"`       | Compression applied to the binary before base64 encoding. Values: `"brotli"` or `"none"`. Use `"none"` for already-compressed binaries. |

**Asset path resolution:** Any string value for `favicon`, `css`, `html`, `scripts`, `binary`, or `glue` that starts with `https://` or `http://` is treated as a remote URL and fetched over HTTP. All other strings are treated as local file paths relative to the working directory where `histos` is invoked.

---

## Minimal Example (CSS + metadata only, no WASM)

```yaml
pack:
  metadata:
    title: "My App"
    author: "Jane Doe"
    description: "A simple static page"
  html:
    - ./index.html
  scripts:
    - ./app.js
  css:
    - ./style.css
  runtime:
    enabled: false
```

No WASM, no runtime decoder needed. Set `runtime.enabled: false` to produce a lean HTML file without the loader infrastructure.

---

## Full Example (WASM app with brotli compression)

```yaml
pack:
  # Runtime is on by default — include all components
  runtime:
    enabled: true
    icon: true
    core: true
    decoder: true

  metadata:
    title: "My WASM App"
    author: "Jane Doe"
    description: "A Rust/WASM application packed by histos"
    keywords: "wasm, rust, offline"

  favicon:
    - ./assets/icon.svg

  css:
    - https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/normalize/8.0.1/normalize.min.css
    - ./styles/app.css

  scripts:
    - ./src/init.js

  wasm:
    app:
      # path points to the wasm-pack project root (parent of pkg/)
      path: ./my-wasm-project
      # id MUST be "bin-wasm-app" when using the default runtime
      id: "bin-wasm-app"
      compile_wasm: false
      compression: "brotli"
```

If you have already run `wasm-pack build --target no-modules`, the `pkg/` directory contains `*_bg.wasm` and `*.js` — histos auto-discovers them from `path`. Set `compile_wasm: true` to have histos run the build step itself.

Alternatively, specify files explicitly without a `path`:

```yaml
  wasm:
    app:
      binary: ./my-wasm-project/pkg/my_app_bg.wasm
      glue: ./my-wasm-project/pkg/my_app.js
      id: "bin-wasm-app"
      compression: "brotli"
```

---

## Output

histos writes a single `index.html` file (default: `./index.html`). The file is self-contained:
- No external network requests at load time
- No server required — open directly in a browser via `file://`
- WASM binary is decoded from base64 and decompressed at runtime by the bundled brotli decoder
- Decoded WASM is cached in IndexedDB to avoid re-decoding on subsequent loads

---

## Common Pitfalls

- **Wrong `id`**: If using `runtime.core = true` (the default), the WASM module `id` **must** be `"bin-wasm-app"`. Any other value means `core.js` will not find the module and the app will not start.
- **`compression: "brotli"` requires the decoder**: If `runtime.decoder = false` but `compression = "brotli"`, the WASM cannot be decoded. Either set `runtime.decoder: true` or use `compression: "none"`.
- **`compile_wasm: true` requires `wasm-pack`**: Install with `cargo install wasm-pack`. If you already have a built `pkg/` directory, keep `compile_wasm: false`.
- **`path` vs `binary`/`glue`**: Use `path` when you have a wasm-pack project directory. Use `binary`/`glue` when you have pre-built files at specific paths or remote URLs. Do not mix both for the same module.
- **`metadata` not `meta`**: The YAML key for metadata is `metadata:`, not `meta:`.
- **Local paths are relative to CWD**: Paths in the config are resolved relative to the directory from which `histos` is invoked, not the config file location.
